root_cause: Sailly voice bot is rejecting WebSocket connections with HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests), indicating rate limiting is either misconfigured or downstream API quotas are exhausted.

fix_actions.description: Inspect Sailly application logs (not system health) for rate limiter configuration, Deepgram API quota status, Claude API rate limit headers, and Gemini TTS quota. Check if rate limiter is defaulting to 0 allowed requests or if downstream API keys have hit daily/monthly limits.

If rate limiter is too aggressive, increase WebSocket connection rate limit threshold (e.g., from 1/s to 10/s) and verify all downstream API credentials (Deepgram, Claude, Gemini) have non-zero quota remaining.
Restart sailly-browser-demo service after config changes to flush any stale rate limiter state or API connection pools.
